description_long: This utility pole is a tall, slender structure with a cylindrical shaft that tapers slightly towards the top and is anchored by a wider, rounded base. At the upper section, a single horizontal crossarm extends perpendicularly from the main pole, supported by a diagonal brace for stability. The crossarm features several insulator mounts spaced evenly along its length, likely for supporting electrical wires. A thick, dark insulator or connector is attached near the junction of the crossarm and the pole. The surface of the pole appears to be metallic or coated, with a dark, weathered finish, while the crossarm and brace have a lighter, possibly wooden or composite appearance. The overall design is typical for urban or suburban power distribution, with no visible transformers, streetlights, or identification tags.
description: A tall, dark utility pole with a single horizontal crossarm, insulator mounts, and a sturdy base, designed for supporting electrical wires in urban settings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the pole, the horizontal crossarm with insulator mounts, and the diagonal brace.
description_view_1: Narrow end-on view highlighting the slim profile of the pole and the rounded base, with the crossarm barely visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the crossarm, insulator mounts, and diagonal brace from the other side.
description_view_3: End-on view from the opposite direction of view 1, again showing the slim profile and rounded base.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: A single horizontal crossarm is mounted near the top of the pole, supported by a diagonal brace.
